requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks -
Interest Rates on Advances) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Interest Rates on Advances) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Commercial Banks (hereinafter collectively
referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')
For the purpose of these Directions, 'Commercial Banks' mean banking companies
(other than Small Finance Banks, Payment Banks, and Local Area Banks),
corresponding new banks, and the State Bank of India, as defined respectively under
clauses (c), (da) and (nc) of Section 5 of the Banking Regulation Act,1949.
requirements.object: Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank') For the purpose of these Directions,

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall not be applicable to operations of foreign branches of Indian
banks.
requirements.object: of foreign branches of Indian banks

requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Advance against own deposit' means advance granted against Rupee/
FCNR(B) term deposit and the deposit stands in the name of:
requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Advance against own deposit' means advance granted against Rupee/ FCNR(B) term deposit and the deposit

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Benchmark Prime Lending Rate (BPLR)' means internal benchmark rate used to
determine the interest rates on advances / loans sanctioned upto June 30, 2010.
requirements.object: to determine the interest rates on advances / loans sanctioned upto June 30, 2010

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(3) 'Benchmark rate' means the reference rate used to determine the interest rates
on loans.
requirements.object: to determine the interest rates on loans

requirements.full_sentence: (4) 'External benchmark rate' means the reference rate which includes:
requirements.object: includes:

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) 'Fixed rate loan' means a loan on which the interest rate is fixed for the entire tenor
of the loan.
requirements.object: the interest rate is fixed for the entire tenor of the loan

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) 'Floating rate loan' means a loan on which interest rate does not remain fixed
during the tenor of the loan.
requirements.object: interest rate does not remain fixed during the tenor of the loan

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) 'Internal benchmark rate' means a reference rate determined internally by the
bank.
requirements.object: internally by the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(8) 'Microfinance loan' shall have the same meaning as assigned in Reserve Bank of
India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as assigned in Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: (10) 'Short term borrowing' means borrowing of tenor up to but less than one year.
requirements.object: to but less than one year

requirements.full_sentence: (11) 'Term loan' means a loan which is repayable after a specified term period.
requirements.object: repayable after a specified term period

requirements.full_sentence:
(12) All other expressions, unless defined herein, shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act,1949 or the Reserve
Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory modification or re-enactment thereto or as
used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act,1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory modi

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall charge interest on its loans and advances on the terms and conditions
specified in these directions.
requirements.object: loans and advances on the terms and conditions specified in these directions

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) There shall be a comprehensive policy duly approved by the Board of Directors or
any committee of the Board to which powers have been delegated, in respect of
interest rates on advances, including the pricing of microfinance loans.
requirements.object: duly approved by the Board of Directors or any committee of the Board to which powers have been delegated, in respect of int

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) All floating rate loans, except those mentioned in paragraph 45, shall be priced
with reference to a benchmark as mentioned in paragraphs 10 to 27.
requirements.object: to a benchmark as mentioned in paragraphs 10 to 27

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall have the freedom to offer all categories of advances on fixed or
floating interest rates.
requirements.object: offer all categories of advances on fixed or floating interest rates

conditions: When the floating rate advances are linked to a reference benchmark rate
requirements.full_sentence:
(4) When the floating rate advances are linked to a reference benchmark rate, a bank
shall determine its actual lending rates by adding the components of spread to the
reference benchmark rate.
requirements.object: rates by adding the components of spread to the reference benchmark rate

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The reference benchmark rate used for pricing the loans shall form part of the
terms of the loan contract.
requirements.object: terms of the loan contract

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Interest rates on fixed rate loans of tenor below three years shall not be less than
the benchmark rate for similar tenor and shall be as per directions contained in
paragraph 45(4)(v).
requirements.object: benchmark rate for similar tenor and shall be as per directions contained in paragraph 45(4)(v)

requirements.full_sentence: (7) Interest shall be charged on all advances at monthly rests.
requirements.object: advances at monthly rests

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that interest on agricultural advances and advance to farmers shall be
charged by a bank as per below instructions:
requirements.object: bank as per below instructions:

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Total interest debited in short term loan should not exceed the principal amount
for advances to small and marginal farmers.
requirements.object: for advances to small and marginal farmers

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) A bank may charge interest on agricultural advances at annual rests for long
duration crop only.
requirements.object: advances at annual rests for long duration crop only

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) As regards other agricultural advances in respect of short duration crops and
allied activities, a bank may take into consideration due date/s fixed on the basis
of fluidity with borrowers and harvesting / marketing season while charging
interest and compound the same if the loan / instalment becomes overdue.
requirements.object: date/s fixed on the basis of fluidity with borrowers and harvesting / marketing season while charging interest and compoun

requirements.full_sentence: (8) Interest chargeable on rupee advances shall be rounded off to the nearest rupee.
requirements.object: the nearest rupee

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(9) Interest charged on small value loans, particularly, personal loans and such other
loans of similar nature shall be justifiable having regard to the total cost incurred by
the bank in extending the loan and the extent of return that could be reasonably
expected from the transaction.
requirements.object: to the total cost incurred by the bank in extending the loan and the extent of return that could be reasonably expected f

conditions:
In case of takeover of bank branches in rural and semi urban centres from one
bank to another bank
requirements.full_sentence:
(10) In case of takeover of bank branches in rural and semi urban centres from one
bank to another bank, transfer of borrowal accounts of the existing branch to the
branch of acquiring bank shall be on mutually agreed terms of contract.
requirements.object: terms of contract

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the existing borrowers shall not be put into any disadvantage and shall
have the option of continuing with the existing bank or the acquiring bank.
requirements.object: disadvantage and shall have the option of continuing with the existing bank or the acquiring bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(11) There shall be no lending below the benchmark rate for a particular maturity for
all loans linked to that benchmark.
requirements.object: the benchmark rate for a particular maturity for all loans linked to that benchmark

requirements.full_sentence:
The directions contained in paragraph 6 above shall also be applicable to Rupee
advances granted against FCNR(B) deposits to a third party or out of resources
mobilised under the FCNR(B) scheme.
requirements.object: Rupee advances granted against FCNR(B) deposits to a third party or out of resources mobilised under the FCNR(B) scheme

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place a Board-approved policy regarding pricing of microfinance
loans which shall, inter alia, cover the following:
(1) A well-documented interest rate model / approach for arriving at the all-inclusive
interest rate.
requirements.object: Board-approved policy regarding pricing of microfinance loans which shall, inter alia, cover the following: (1) A well-documented inter

requirements.full_sentence:
Interest rates and other charges / fees on microfinance loans should not be
usurious.
These shall be subjected to supervisory scrutiny by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: scrutiny by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
All floating rate rupee loans sanctioned and renewed between July 1, 2010 and
March 31, 2016 shall be priced with reference to the Base Rate which will be the
internal benchmark for such purposes.
requirements.object: to the Base Rate which will be the internal benchmark for such purposes

requirements.full_sentence:
Base Rate shall include all those elements of the lending rates that are common
across all categories of borrowers.
requirements.object: of the lending rates that are common across all categories of borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall have the freedom to calculate cost of funds either on the basis of
average cost of funds or on marginal cost of funds or any other methodology in vogue,
which is reasonable and transparent, subject to it being consistent and made available
for supervisory review / scrutiny as and when required.
requirements.object: calculate cost of funds either on the basis of average cost of funds or on marginal cost of funds or any other methodology in vogue, which is reasonable and transparent

conditions: Provided that where the card rate for deposits of one or more tenor is the basis
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that where the card rate for deposits of one or more tenor is the basis, the
deposits in the chosen tenor/s shall have the largest share in the deposit base of the
bank.
requirements.object: in the deposit base of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall review the Base Rate at least once in a quarter with the approval of
the Board or the Asset Liability Management Committees (ALCOs) as per the bank's
practice.
requirements.object: at least once in a quarter with the approval of the Board or the Asset Liability Management Committees (ALCOs) as per the bank's

requirements.full_sentence:
April 1, 2016 shall be
priced with reference to the Marginal Cost of Funds based Lending Rate (MCLR) which
will be the internal benchmark for such purposes subject to the provisions contained
in paragraphs 25 to 27.
requirements.object: to the Marginal Cost of Funds based Lending Rate (MCLR) which will be the internal benchmark for such purposes subject to the

requirements.full_sentence:
The MCLR shall comprise of:
(1) Marginal cost of funds;
requirements.object: cost of funds

requirements.full_sentence:
The marginal cost of funds shall comprise of Marginal cost of borrowings and
return on networth.
requirements.object: of borrowings and return on networth

requirements.full_sentence:
Negative carry on the mandatory CRR which arises due to return on CRR
balances being nil, will be calculated as under:
Required CRR x (marginal cost) / (1- CRR)
The marginal cost of funds arrived at paragraph 17 above shall be used for arriving at
negative carry on CRR.
requirements.object: at negative carry on CRR

requirements.full_sentence:
All operating costs associated with providing the loan product including cost of
raising funds shall be included under this head.
requirements.object: head

requirements.full_sentence:
It shall be ensured that the costs of
providing those services which are separately recovered by way of service charges do
not form part of this component.
requirements.object: costs of providing those services which are separately recovered by way of service charges do not form part of this component

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall calculate all operating costs as a percentage of marginal cost of funds
for computing MCLR.
requirements.object: as a percentage of marginal cost of funds for computing MCLR

requirements.full_sentence:
The change in tenor
premium should not be borrower specific or loan class specific.
requirements.object: loan class specific

requirements.full_sentence:
The tenor of the MCLR calculated as per the Annex shall correspond to the
following:
(1) the tenor of the funds in the single largest maturity bucket, provided it is more than
30 percent of the entire funds (other than equity) reckoned for determining the MCLR,
or
(2) the weighted average tenor of two or more maturity buckets that together account
for more than 30 percent, if no single maturity bucket accounts for more than 30
<image>
percent of the funds.
requirements.object: (1) the tenor of the funds in the single largest maturity bucket

requirements.full_sentence:
The maturity bucket shall be arrived at by calculating the
cumulative weightage based on the descending order of the maturity time buckets.
requirements.object: calculating the cumulative weightage based on the descending order of the maturity time buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
Original Maturity
(other than equity)
(in percent)
1 5 years & above 15.1 15.1
2 3 years & above but less than 5 years 11.8 26.9
3 2 years & above but less than 3 years 9.3 36.2
4 1 year & above but less than 2 years 16.9 53.1
5 6 months & above but less than 1 year 24.3 77.4
6 91 days & above but less than 6 months 10.5 87.9
7 Up to 90 days 12.1 100
Total 100
For above-mentioned maturity profile of borrowings, the MCLR shall correspond to the
weighted average of tenor of the first three time buckets.
requirements.object: average of tenor of the first three time buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
Since MCLR will be a tenor linked benchmark, a bank shall arrive at the MCLR of
various maturities by incorporating the corresponding tenor premium / discount to the
sum of Marginal cost of funds, Negative carry on account of CRR and Operating costs.
requirements.object: of various maturities by incorporating the corresponding tenor premium / discount to the sum of Marginal cost of funds, Negative ca

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, a bank shall publish the internal benchmark for the following maturities:
(1) overnight MCLR,
(2) one-month MCLR,
(3) three-month MCLR,
(4) six month MCLR,
(5) One year MCLR.
requirements.object: for the following maturities: (1) overnight MCLR, (2) one-month MCLR, (3) three-month MCLR, (4) six month MCLR, (5) One

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the above, a bank shall have the option of publishing MCLR of any other
longer maturity.
requirements.object: publishing MCLR of any other longer maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall review and publish its MCLR of different maturities every month on a
pre-announced date with the approval of the Board or any other committee to which
powers have been delegated.
requirements.object: MCLR of different maturities every month on a pre-announced date with the approval of the Board or any other committee to which

requirements.full_sentence:
All floating rate personal or retail loans (housing, auto, etc.), and floating rate loans
extended by banks to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) shall be
benchmarked to an External Benchmark Rate.
requirements.object: External Benchmark Rate
benchmark within a loan category

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall have a Board approved policy delineating the components of spread
charged to a customer.
requirements.object: policy delineating the components of spread charged to a customer

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall include principles:
(1) To determine the quantum of each component of spread.
requirements.object: determine the quantum of each component of spread

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the conditions laid down in paragraph 28 of these Directions, a bank
shall adhere to the following conditions:
(1) The credit risk premium charged to an existing borrower shall not be increased
except on account of deterioration in the credit risk profile of the customer or change
in tenor premium.
requirements.object: account of deterioration in the credit risk profile of the customer or change in tenor premium

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the stipulation contained in paragraph 29(1) above shall not be
applicable to loans under consortium / multiple banking arrangements.
requirements.object: under consortium / multiple banking arrangements

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The change in tenor premium on loans sanctioned under Base rate system shall
not be borrower specific or loan class specific.
requirements.object: loan class specific

requirements.full_sentence:
In other words, the change in tenor
premium shall be uniform for all types of loans for a given residual tenor.
requirements.object: types of loans for a given residual tenor

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the spread guidelines mentioned above shall not apply to loans granted
under BPLR system, which continue till date.
requirements.object: under BPLR system, which continue till date

requirements.full_sentence:
Such loans shall be covered under the
terms of the loan agreements.
requirements.object: terms of the loan agreements

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the conditions laid down in paragraph 28 of these Directions, a bank
shall adopt the following broad components of spread:
(1) Business strategy
The component shall be arrived at taking into consideration the business strategy,
market competition, embedded options in the loan product, market liquidity of the loan
etc.
requirements.object: components of spread: (1) Business strategy The component shall be arrived at taking into consideration the business strateg

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Credit risk premium
The credit risk premium charged to the customer representing the default risk arising
<image>
from loan sanctioned shall be arrived at based on an appropriate credit risk rating /
scoring model and after taking into consideration customer relationship, expected
losses, collaterals, etc.
requirements.object: on an appropriate credit risk rating / scoring model and after taking into consideration customer relationship, expected losses, c

requirements.full_sentence:
The spread charged to an existing borrower shall not be increased except on
account of deterioration in the credit risk profile of the customer.
requirements.object: account of deterioration in the credit risk profile of the customer

requirements.full_sentence:
Any such decision
regarding change in spread on account of change in credit risk profile shall be
supported by a full-fledged risk profile review of the customer.
requirements.object: full-fledged risk profile review of the customer

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the stipulation contained in paragraph 31 above shall not be applicable
to loans under consortium / multiple banking arrangements.
requirements.object: under consortium / multiple banking arrangements

requirements.full_sentence:
business strategy and Credit risk premium shall
have either a positive value or be zero.
requirements.object: value or be zero

conditions:
when borrower's credit assessment
undergoes a substantial change
requirements.full_sentence:
However, credit
risk premium may undergo change only when borrower's credit assessment
undergoes a substantial change, as agreed upon in the loan contract.
requirements.object: borrower's credit assessment undergoes a substantial change, as agreed upon in the loan contract

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the other spread components may be reduced by banks for a loan
category earlier than three years for customer retention, on justifiable grounds, in a
non-discriminatory manner, and in terms of the bank's policy.
requirements.object: may be reduced by banks for a loan category earlier than three years for customer retention, on justifiable grounds, in a

requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall, at its option, specify interest reset dates on its floating rate loans.
requirements.object: specify interest reset dates on its floating rate loans

requirements.full_sentence:
A
bank shall have the option to offer loans with reset dates linked either to the date of
first disbursement of the loan / credit limits or to the date of review of MCLR.
requirements.object: offer loans with reset dates linked either to the date of first disbursement of the loan / credit limits or to the date of review o

requirements.full_sentence:
The MCLR prevailing on the date of first disbursement, whether partial or full, shall
be applicable till the next reset date, irrespective of the changes in the benchmark
during the interim.
requirements.object: next reset date, irrespective of the changes in the benchmark during the interim

requirements.full_sentence: Future reset dates shall be determined accordingly.
The periodicity of reset shall be one year or lower.
requirements.object: lower

requirements.full_sentence:
The exact periodicity of reset
shall form part of the terms of the loan contract.
requirements.object: terms of the loan contract

requirements.full_sentence:
The periodicity of the reset under MCLR shall correspond to the tenor / maturity of
the MCLR to which the loan is linked.
requirements.object: / maturity of the MCLR to which the loan is linked

requirements.full_sentence:
The interest rate under external benchmark shall be reset at least once in three
months.
requirements.object: once in three months

requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall continue to review and publish Base Rate as hitherto.
requirements.object: publish Base Rate as hitherto

requirements.full_sentence:
Existing loans and credit limits linked to the Base Rate / BPLR shall continue till
repayment or renewal, as the case may be.
requirements.object: renewal, as the case may be

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that existing borrowers shall have the option to move to the MCLR linked
loan at mutually acceptable terms.
requirements.object: move to the MCLR linked loan at mutually acceptable terms

conditions: Provided that the switch-over shall not be treated as a foreclosure of existing facility
requirements.full_sentence: Provided that the switch-over shall not be treated as a foreclosure of existing facility.
requirements.object: foreclosure of existing facility

requirements.full_sentence:
Existing loans and credit limits linked to the MCLR / Base Rate / BPLR shall
continue till repayment or renewal, as the case may be.
requirements.object: renewal, as the case may be

conditions: Provided that floating rate term loans sanctioned to borrowers who
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that floating rate term loans sanctioned to borrowers who, in terms of extant
guidelines, are eligible to prepay a floating rate loan without pre-payment charges,
shall be eligible for switchover to External Benchmark without any charges / fees,
except reasonable administrative / legal costs.
requirements.object: to External Benchmark without any charges / fees

requirements.full_sentence:
The final rate charged to this category
of borrowers, post switchover to external benchmark, shall be same as the rate
charged for a new loan of the same category, type, tenor and amount, at the time of
origination of the loan.
requirements.object: rate charged for a new loan of the same category, type, tenor and amount, at the time of origination of the loan

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that other existing borrowers shall have the option to move to External
Benchmark at mutually acceptable terms.
requirements.object: move to External Benchmark at mutually acceptable terms

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall have the freedom to determine the interest rates on advances in
foreign currency as per the comprehensive policy on interest rates on advances duly
approved by the Board of Directors or any committee of the Board to which powers
have been delegated.
requirements.object: determine the interest rates on advances in foreign currency as per the comprehensive policy on interest rates on advances duly ap

requirements.full_sentence:
The interest rates shall be determined with reference to a market determined
external benchmark.
requirements.object: to a market determined external benchmark

requirements.full_sentence:
The actual lending rates shall be determined by adding the components of spread
to the external benchmark.
requirements.object: the components of spread to the external benchmark

requirements.full_sentence:
The following types of loans shall be exempted from the provisions contained
under paragraphs 10 to 41 of these Directions:
(1) Loans covered by schemes specially formulated by Government of India wherein
a bank has to charge interest rates as per the scheme.
requirements.object: provisions contained under paragraphs 10 to 41 of these Directions: (1) Loans covered by schemes specially formulated by Governme

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Loans granted under various refinance schemes formulated by Government of
India or any Government Undertakings wherein a bank charges interest at the rates
prescribed under the schemes to the extent refinance is available, Interest rate
charged on the part not covered under refinance shall adhere to the Base rate / MCLR
/ External Benchmark guidelines.
requirements.object: rate / MCLR / External Benchmark guidelines

conditions:
Provided that floating rate loans based on external benchmark sanctioned before
April 01
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that floating rate loans based on external benchmark sanctioned before
April 01, 2016 shall be equal to or above the Base Rate at the time of sanction or
renewal.
requirements.object: above the Base Rate at the time of sanction or renewal

conditions:
in case of hybrid loans where the interest rates are partly fixed and
partly floating
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that in case of hybrid loans where the interest rates are partly fixed and
partly floating, interest rate on the floating portion shall be subject to relevant
floating interest rate instructions as contained in these Directions.
requirements.object: floating interest rate instructions as contained in these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that interest rates for fixed rate loans (including fixed rate portion
of hybrid loans) of tenor up to three years (except interest rates on bill discounting
and factoring facilities) shall not be less than the sum of following:
requirements.object: sum of following:

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Provided further that fixed rate loans sanctioned before April 01, 2016 shall not be
below the Base Rate at the time of sanction or renewal.
requirements.object: Rate at the time of sanction or renewal

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions and guidelines
repealed prior to the issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Further,
the repeal of these directions, instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way
prejudicially affect:
(1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder;
requirements.object: affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

requirements.full_sentence:
and
any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be
imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in
force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
<image>
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of these
Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.

A Marginal Cost of Borrowings
1 Deposits
a Current Deposits


The core portion of current deposits identified based on the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks
- Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025 should be reckoned for arriving at the balance outstanding.
requirements.object: at the balance outstanding

requirements.full_sentence:
b Savings
The core portion of savings deposits identified based on the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks
- Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025 should be reckoned for arriving at the balance outstanding.
requirements.object: at the balance outstanding

requirements.full_sentence:
c Term deposits (Fixed Rate)
Deposits
Term deposits of various maturities including those on which differential interest rates are payable should be included.

d Term deposits (Floating Rate)
The rate should be arrived at based on the prevailing external benchmark rate on the date of review.
requirements.object: on the prevailing external benchmark rate on the date of review

requirements.full_sentence:
e Foreign currency
Foreign currency deposits, to the extent deployed for lending in rupees, should be included in computing marginal cost of funds.
requirements.object: marginal cost of funds

requirements.full_sentence:
The swap cost and hedge
deposits
<image>
cost of such deposits should be reckoned for computing marginal cost.
requirements.object: marginal cost

requirements.full_sentence:
c Foreign
Borrowings
Foreign currency borrowings, to the extent deployed for lending in rupees, should be included in computing marginal cost of funds.
requirements.object: marginal cost of funds

requirements.full_sentence:
Marginal cost of
Currency Borrowings including HO borrowings by foreign banks (other than those forming part of Tier-I capital)
The marginal cost of borrowings shall have a weightage of 92 percent of Marginal Cost of Funds while return on networth will have the balance weightage of 8 percent.
requirements.object: 92 percent of Marginal Cost of Funds while return on networth will have the balance weightage of 8 percent

requirements.full_sentence:
B Return on networth
borrowings
Amount of common equity Tier 1 capital required to be maintained for Risk Weighted Assets as per extant capital adequacy norms shall be included for computing marginal cost of funds.
requirements.object: marginal cost of funds

conditions: where lending operations are mainly financed by capital
requirements.full_sentence: In case of newly set up banks (either domestic or foreign banks operating as branches in India) where lending operations are mainly financed by capital, the weightage for this component may be higher, i.e., in proportion to the extent of capital deployed for lending.

Marginal cost of funds = 92 percent x Marginal cost of borrowings + 8 percent x
Return on networth
Note: A bank shall have the option to reckon the outstanding balances of deposits and
other borrowings as on any day, not more than seven calendar days, prior to the date
from which the MCLR becomes effective.
requirements.object: reckon the outstanding balances of deposits and other borrowings as on any day, not more than seven calendar days, prior to the dat

requirements.full_sentence:
The chosen time lag shall be maintained
consistently for a period not less one year.
requirements.object: a period not less one year
